pytorch.reason: All 8 new coupling cell computations use pytorch complex128 tensors: density matrices, Kraus channels (depolarizing, dephasing), partial traces, fiber phase entropy, chirality entropy, off-diagonal norm, quantum discord. L2_on_L1, L5_on_L1, L4_on_L1, L1_on_L2, L1_on_L5, L2_on_L5, L4_on_L3, L3_on_L2 all pytorch-native.

pyg.reason: not needed -- no message passing in this sim

z3.reason: Encodes UNSAT proofs for any DESTROY cells found; encodes SAT proof for depolarizing-vs-dephasing fiber distinction (L5_on_L1 != L3_on_L1); encodes SAT proof for Werner-CPT invariance (L2_on_L5=PRESERVE).

cvc5.reason: not needed -- z3 covers incompatibility proofs

sympy.reason: Closed-form derivation of rho_01 after dephasing vs depolarizing; proves analytically that dephasing DESTROYS phi (rho_01=0 at p=0.5) while depolarizing PRESERVES phi direction (rho_01 scaled but nonzero); this is the key distinction between L3_on_L1 and L5_on_L1.

clifford.reason: Verifies CPT operator in Cl(3): e12 (left-chirality bivector) maps to e21=-e12 (right-chirality) under e1 reflection; confirms L<->R swap is a Cl(3) rotor operation, grounding L2 operator in geometric algebra.

geomstats.reason: not needed -- Hopf geometry via Cl(3) rotors

e3nn.reason: not needed -- equivariance via Clifford SU(2)

rustworkx.reason: Updated directed coupling graph with all 19 known cells (11 prior + 8 new); SCCs identify mutually compatible layer pairs; partial order check determines if a total constraint ordering exists across all 5 layers.

xgi.reason: not needed -- directed graph not hypergraph

toponetx.reason: not needed -- layer topology via rustworkx

L2_on_L1.note: CPT=X⊗X on (Hopf⊗|0><0|): X acts on qubit1, maps |+phi>->|-phi> since X flips |0><->|1>. Fiber phase phi -> pi-phi under X (maps cos/sin). The fiber distribution shifts but remains uniform -> entropy preserved.

L5_on_L1.note: Depolarizing D_p=0.5: rho_01 -> 0.5*rho_01 (not zero, unlike Z-dephasing). Fiber phase phi still accessible from off-diagonal argument; magnitude halved but phi direction preserved -> distribution may still be uniform.

L4_on_L1.note: Fe relay acts on BC only (partial-SWAP BC); qubit A is causally isolated. By quantum data-processing: tracing out BC after local BC operation leaves rho_A identical. Fiber entropy of A should be preserved exactly.

L1_on_L2.note: SU(2) X-rotation on qubit 1 mixes |0>(L) and |1>(R) components. |LL>=|00> -> superposition of L and R on qubit 1; chirality projector P_L now sees mixed population -> chirality entropy changes.

L1_on_L5.note: Werner state |psi-> is SU(2)⊗SU(2)-invariant (singlet has zero angular momentum). Local R⊗I changes the measurement basis for discord computation but quantum discord is basis-independent by definition -> should PRESERVE.

L2_on_L5.note: Werner singlet |psi-> = (|01>-|10>)/sqrt(2) under X⊗X: X|0>=|1>, X|1>=|0>, so |01>-|10> -> |10>-|01> = -(|01>-|10>). Density matrix unchanged (phase^2=1). I/4 invariant under any unitary. So rho_W -> rho_W exactly -> PRESERVE.

L4_on_L3.note: Z-dephasing of qubit A collapses its off-diagonal. Fe relay (partial-SWAP on BC) cannot transfer coherence TO A because it acts on the orthogonal BC subspace. Data processing: local ops on BC cannot increase coherence in A. Off-diagonal norm of A preserved (both near zero) -> classify as NULL or PRESERVE.

L3_on_L2.note: Mixed chiral state 0.5|LL><LL|+0.5|RR><RR| is block-diagonal in L/R basis which IS the Z eigenstate basis. Z-dephasing kills off-diagonals but this state has zero off-diagonals already -> rho unchanged -> PRESERVE.

neg_identity_preserves_fiber_entropy.note: Identity channel must preserve fiber entropy exactly

neg_full_depolarizing_destroys_fiber.note: Full depolarizing -> I/2 for all states; phi info lost -> fiber entropy should DESTROY

neg_z_deph_p0_identity_on_chirality.note: Z-dephasing at p=0 is identity; chirality entropy must not change

boundary_depolarizing_p_sweep_hopf.note: Depolarizing p sweep: shows at what p fiber entropy degrades vs DESTROY threshold

boundary_cpt_hopf_equator_pi2.note: CPT on Hopf fiber at equator_pi2; fiber entropy changes characterize CPT coupling

boundary_cpt_hopf_pole_pi.note: CPT on Hopf fiber at pole_pi; fiber entropy changes characterize CPT coupling

no_new_destroy_cells.note: No new DESTROY cells found. Degrade cells: []

depolarizing_vs_dephasing_fiber_distinct.note: Formal distinction: depolarizing does NOT zero off-diagonal (SAT: entropy can survive); dephasing DOES zero off-diagonal (UNSAT: entropy cannot survive). Measured L5_on_L1=PRESERVE vs known L3_on_L1=DESTROY.

z3_werner_cpt_invariance.note: Formally: CPT preserves singlet density matrix (phase squares to 1) and preserves I/4 (unitary invariance). Werner = convex combo of both. Therefore Werner preserved -> discord preserved. SAT confirms logical consistency.

test_10_sympy_depolarizing_vs_dephasing.conclusion: Z-dephasing (p=0.5): rho_01 -> (1-2*0.5)*c*s*e^{-i*phi} = 0. Phi DESTROYED. Depolarizing (p=0.5): rho_01 -> (1-0.5)*c*s*e^{-i*phi} = c*s/2 * e^{-i*phi}. Phi PRESERVED (halved amplitude). This proves L5_on_L1 != L3_on_L1 analytically: depolarizing degrades/preserves fiber entropy; dephasing destroys it entirely.

clifford_cpt_verification.note: CPT as Cl(3) bivector swap: e12 (L-chirality) -> e21 = -e12 (R-chirality). Verified via: e1*e12*e1 = -e12, consistent with L<->R reflection in Cl(3). This grounds the CPT operator in geometric algebra.
